title: dryopteris phegopteris var. cruciata
intro: Dryopteris phegopteris var. cruciata, also known as the Cross-leaved Shield Fern, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Dryopteris phegopteris plant species, typically found in woodland environments with moist, shaded conditions. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits associated with the Cross-leaved Shield Fern include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
